archetypes.archetype: parse-reply mutation path (highest-risk: the only path that writes human judgments)

checks.evidence: Reply grammar implemented executably (review-console.html, logic mirrors spec §6.5) and exercised with 15 assertions in node: pass all / fail+pass rest / na-without-justification rejected / superseded row rejected (INV-10) / fail all rejected / bare US-n rejected / duplicate-verdict rejected / unknown row rejected / judged-pass immutability (INV-13) / mixed valid+invalid reply leaves state unchanged (atomicity, INV-A3). All pass.

Re-traced 2026-06-11 against FINAL spec §7: raw update-file payload → module-side sender/message/text extraction → sender allowlist (INV-A4) → grammar validation → batch active check → row-hash staleness check → reply_ref dedup (INV-17) → lock acquire → atomic apply with provenance (INV-A7). Sender check precedes any mutation; lock wraps full read-modify-write (INV-A2).

Re-traced 2026-06-11: parse-reply is one of exactly 8 mutators (CB-6/DD-1 named-owner expansion); judgments enter the ledger at parse time; no intermediate judgment store exists (spec §7); emit-system-validation is read-only projection (INV-A1).

Re-traced 2026-06-11: invalid reply → RepromptRequired, zero mutations, exit 2; lock timeout → exit 3 LEDGER_BUSY; corrupt ledger → exit 3 LEDGER_CORRUPT with bytes copied aside to .corrupt-<ts>, no auto-repair; every invocation prints one stdout JSON envelope {status, code, issues, data} (FM-5; INV-A3).

archetypes.archetype: digest batch lifecycle incl. crash/re-entry (highest-risk: external service + human in the loop + close idempotency)

checks.evidence: Re-traced 2026-06-11 against FINAL spec: assemble-digest refuses while a non-terminal batch exists; batch snapshots conops/row/evidence hashes; record-send records per-part delivery, batch flips to sent only when all parts recorded (RC-2); bulk verdicts gated on fully-sent batch (INV-16); reply_ref idempotency rejects edited-message re-application (INV-17); multi-part split at 3500 chars at row boundaries, each part labeled digest-id + i/k; stale digest-id and changed-hash replies rejected at parse. At-least-once messaging + parse-layer rejection = effectively-once judgment application (INV-A6).

Close re-entry walked at every step: all judged → NOTHING_TO_DIGEST skips to emission; clean emission exists → skip to MCP call; card metadata already system_validation_complete → skip MCP entirely. Crash between emission and MCP call re-enters cleanly; no re-judging path exists (spec §8 close-step idempotency).

Re-traced 2026-06-11 (SEC-1 Jason-ruled shape): parse-reply --source telegram reads the RAW wake-listener payload via --update-file; the module extracts sender id itself (conductor never transcribes identity); checked against registry allowed_sender_ids, distinct from chat_id (SEC-2); missing/malformed registry fails closed (ALLOWLIST_CONFIG_INVALID); non-allowlisted discarded with hashed-sender security event, no re-prompt. Outbound: secret deny-pattern lint blocks assembly; digest carries scenario/oracle/evidence summaries only; evidence stays local by path reference. Telegram-down fallback: cancel-batch + terminal AskUserQuestion for the whole batch, same grammar + provenance (INV-1).

All 7 INV-A* exercised by at least one archetype check above (A1:sot_owner, A2/A3:cli path, A4:security_boundary, A5:enforcement via self-check-before-MCP ordering in close trace, A6:delivery+idempotency, A7:provenance in apply step). Each invariant has a linked TC-INV test in tests-pseudo.md.

residual_risks: Module cannot prove evidence-transcript authenticity — conductor discipline + dogfood spot-review (documented honestly, spec §6.3)
Direct ledger edits outside the module — single-user discipline, flagged in matrix bypass risks
OQ-1 (gate tolerance of unknown row fields) — implementation-time verification with specified fallback protocol
reconciliation_note: Re-traced 2026-06-11 against spec-output.md (FINAL) during Phase 7 drift reconciliation; all checks re-verified pass.
